0.original: It's an excellent way to move your spirit. 2 00:00:05,360 --> 00:00:08,290 Now is the magic word of success. 3 00:00:08,480 --> 00:00:17,010 Tomorrow, next week, later, sometime, someday, often as not are synonyms for the failure word, never. 4 00:00:17,520 --> 00:00:22,370 Lots of good dreams never come true because we say, I'll start someday, 5 00:00:22,480 --> 00:00:26,209 when we should say, I'll start now, right now. 6 00:00:27,360 --> 00:00:29,730 Take one example, saving money. 7 00:00:30,000 --> 00:00:34,209 Just about everybody agrees that saving money is a good idea. 8 00:00:34,560 --> 00:00:41,810 But just because it's a good idea doesn't mean many folks follow an organized savings and investment program. 9 00:00:42,240 --> 00:00:48,370 Many people have intentions to save, but only relatively few act on these intentions. 10 00:00:49,200 --> 00:00:54,689 Here's how one young couple got into gear with a regular wealth-accumulation program. 11 00:00:55,120 --> 00:01:03,330 Bill's take-home income was $1,000 a month, but he and his wife Janet spent $1,000 each month too. 12 00:01:03,840 --> 00:01:09,250 Both wanted to save, but there were always reasons why they felt they couldn't begin. 13 00:01:09,520 --> 00:01:13,969 For years, they had promised themselves, we'll start when we get a raise, 14 00:01:14,159 --> 00:01:18,050 when we've caught up with our installments, when we're over the hump, 15 00:01:18,159 --> 00:01:20,290 next month, next year. 16 00:01:21,120 --> 00:01:24,929 Finally, Janet got disgusted with their failure to save. 17 00:01:25,040 --> 00:01:28,850 She said to Bill, look, do we want to save or don't we? 18 00:01:29,040 --> 00:01:35,169 He replied, of course we do, but you know as well as I we can't put aside anything now. 19 00:01:36,000 --> 00:01:39,330 But for once, Janet was in a do-or-die mood. 20 00:01:39,600 --> 00:01:44,050 We've been telling ourselves for years we're going to start a savings program. 21 00:01:44,240 --> 00:01:47,090 We don't save because we think we can't. 22 00:01:47,120 --> 00:01:49,250 Now let's start thinking we can. 23 00:01:49,440 --> 00:01:53,890 I saw an ad today that shows if we'd save just $100 a month, 24 00:01:54,000 --> 00:02:01,250 in 15 years we'd have $18,000 plus $6,600 accumulated interest. 25 00:02:01,520 --> 00:02:09,730 The ad also said it's easier to spend what's left over after savings than it is to save what's left over after spending. 26 00:02:10,080 --> 00:02:15,250 If you're game, let's start with 10% of your pay and let's save off the top. 27 00:02:15,440 --> 00:02:20,290 We may eat crackers and milk before the month's up, but if we have to, we will. 28 00:02:20,960 --> 00:02:26,610 Bill and Janet were cramped for a few months, but soon they were adjusted to their new budget. 29 00:02:26,800 --> 00:02:33,810 Now they feel it's just as much fun to spend money on savings as it is to spend it on something else. 30 00:02:34,560 --> 00:02:36,610 Want to write a note to a friend? 31 00:02:36,800 --> 00:02:37,810 Do it now. 32 00:02:37,999 --> 00:02:40,610 Got an idea you think would help your business? 33 00:02:40,720 --> 00:02:41,890 Present it now. 34 00:02:42,159 --> 00:02:44,689 Live the advice of Benjamin Franklin. 35 00:02:44,800 --> 00:02:48,370 Don't put off until tomorrow what you can do today. 36 00:02:49,120 --> 00:02:59,010 Remember, thinking in terms of now gets things accomplished, but thinking in terms of someday or sometime usually means failure.
0.b1: It is a great way to motivate yourself.
0.cn: 这是一种激励自己的绝佳方式。
0.ai_notes:
'move your spirit': to inspire or motivate someone to take action.
'magic word': a phrase used to describe something that has a powerful or transformative effect.
'often as not': a common way to say 'frequently' or 'more often than not'.
'synonyms': words that have the same or very similar meanings.
'come true': to become a reality, usually referring to dreams or goals.
'folks': an informal, friendly way to refer to people.
'get into gear': to start working effectively or to begin a process with energy.
'take-home income': the amount of money a person actually receives after taxes and other deductions.
'caught up with': to finish work or payments that were previously delayed.
'over the hump': to have passed the most difficult part of a task or situation.
'get disgusted': to feel strong annoyance or unhappiness with a situation.
'put aside': to save money for a future purpose.
'do-or-die': a situation where you must take extreme action to succeed because there is no other choice.
'accumulated interest': money earned on savings over time that is added to the original amount.
'if you're game': an informal way to ask if someone is willing or ready to participate in a plan.
'save off the top': to put money into savings immediately after receiving income, before paying for other expenses.
'cramped': feeling limited or restricted, often used here to describe having very little money to spend.
'put off': to delay or postpone doing something until a later time.
'Benjamin Franklin': one of the Founding Fathers of the United States, famous for his practical advice and wisdom.
0.remarks:
'move your spirit': 激励或鼓舞某人采取行动。
'magic word': 用来形容具有强大或变革性影响的词语。
'often as not': 表达“经常”或“多半”的常用说法。
'synonyms': 意思相同或非常相近的词。
'come true': 成为现实，通常指梦想或目标。
'folks': 对人们的一种非正式、友好的称呼。
'get into gear': 开始高效工作或充满活力地开启某项进程。
'take-home income': 扣除税款和其他费用后，个人实际收到的收入。
'caught up with': 完成之前耽搁的工作或支付欠款。
'over the hump': 已经度过了任务或处境中最困难的部分。
'get disgusted': 对某种情况感到强烈的厌烦或不快。
'put aside': 为未来的目的存钱。
'do-or-die': 必须采取极端行动才能成功的处境，因为别无选择。
'accumulated interest': 储蓄随时间推移产生的利息，并加到原始金额中。
'if you're game': 一种非正式的问法，询问某人是否愿意或准备好参与某项计划。
'save off the top': 在支付其他费用之前，收到收入后立即存钱。
'cramped': 感到受限或局促，此处常用来形容手头非常拮据。
'put off': 推迟或延后做某事。
'Benjamin Franklin': 美国国父之一，以其实用的建议和智慧而闻名。
